column2: CIA triad — three legs (one line each)
column3: Confidentiality = who can read; Integrity = whether it changed; Availability = whether you can use it when needed.

column2: {{c1::Confidentiality}} = read control; {{c2::Integrity}} = change trust; {{c3::Availability}} = timely use.
column3: Story: letter / altered / mailbox opens on schedule.

column2: Ransomware encrypts files; attacker exfiltrated copies first. Which CIA legs?
column3: Availability (can't use files), Integrity (ciphertext), Confidentiality if data left the building.
Identification → Authentication → Authorization → Accountability.

column2: Two passwords on one account — MFA?
column3: No — same factor category (knowledge).
SLE = Asset Value × Exposure Factor; ALE = SLE × Annualized Rate of Occurrence (ARO).
SLE $50k; ALE $100k.
Avoid, Mitigate, Transfer, Accept.
Technical, Administrative/Managerial, Physical.
Preventive, Detective, Corrective; also Deterrent, Recovery, Compensating, Directive.

column2: Guard watching live CCTV — functional control?
column3: Detective (while monitoring). Visible cameras alone = deterrent.
Protect society, the common good, necessary public trust and confidence, and infrastructure.

column2: When ISC2 canons conflict — who wins?
column3: Lower-numbered canon wins: society → honor → principals → profession.
Guideline only — policy, standard, and procedure are mandatory.
Read the letter (C), check it wasn't altered (I), mailbox opens on schedule (A).

column2: BC vs DR vs IR — nest in one sentence
column3: BC keeps the business running; DR restores IT after disaster; IR handles security events (innermost).
RPO = how much data you can afford to lose; RTO = how fast service must return.
Maximum Tolerable Downtime — longest outage the business can survive.

column2: Leadership asks "how much reporting can we lose?" — which metric?
column3: RPO (recovery point). "When are we back?" = RTO.

column2: Backup types — full vs incremental vs differential
column3: Full = everything; Incremental = since last backup of any type; Differential = since last full.

column2: DR site tiers — hot / warm / cold (RTO trend)
column3: Hot ≈ minutes; Warm = hours; Cold = days; offsite tapes = weeks.
Prep kit → smell smoke → close doors → remove cause → reopen kitchen → update runbook.

column2: NIST IR order: Preparation → {{c1::Detection & Analysis}} → {{c2::Containment}} → {{c3::Eradication}} → Recovery → Post-incident.
column3: Exam trap: contain before eradicate.

column2: Phishing at 9 a.m. — which program owns the playbook?
column3: Incident Response (inside BC/DR nesting).
Least privilege, Separation of duties, Need-to-know, Defense in depth.

column2: DAC vs MAC vs RBAC vs ABAC — who decides?
column3: DAC = owner; MAC = system labels; RBAC = job role; ABAC = attributes/rules.

column2: Google Doc owner sets viewer vs commenter — model?
column3: DAC (discretionary). Enterprise group policy adds RBAC.
Two or more different factor categories: knowledge, possession, inherence — not two passwords.
Perimeter → facility → internal secure areas → workstation.
Layer 2 Data Link (not "data in general").
All People Seem To Need Data Processing — Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical.
Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away.
995 = POP3S (high port TLS partner pattern).

column2: SSH port and why it's special
column3: Port 22 — designed secure; replaces Telnet (23) and carries SFTP/SCP.
SYN → SYN/ACK → ACK.

column2: DDoS against auth endpoint — CIA leg?
column3: Availability.

column2: Cloud shared responsibility — you always own
column3: Your data and access policies; provider owns hypervisor/physical per model.

column2: Symmetric vs asymmetric vs hashing jobs
column3: Symmetric = bulk encrypt; Asymmetric = key exchange/signatures; Hashing = integrity (one-way).
Cryptographic hash (SHA-256) — not reversible encryption.
Clear → Purge → Destroy.

column2: Why NTP is a security control
column3: Synchronized clocks enable log correlation and forensics.
Tailgating = no consent; piggybacking = authorized person holds door.
Fabricated scenario / false identity giving the request a plausible reason.

column2: Fake IT calls for password "security check" — technique?
column3: Pretexting (+ often vishing if phone).
